quickAnswer: Take Silenor 3mg within 30 minutes of bedtime, but do not take it within 3 hours of a meal.
Warfarin can be taken with or without food, but keep vitamin K foods like leafy greens consistent.
Metformin is usually taken with meals to reduce stomach upset.
Take levothyroxine on an empty stomach with water, usually 30 to 60 minutes before breakfast.
Take alendronate first thing in the morning with plain water, before any food, drink, or other medicine.
Avoid grapefruit with simvastatin unless your prescriber specifically says it is okay.
Ciprofloxacin can be taken with or without food, but separate it from dairy, calcium, iron, magnesium, zinc, and antacids.
Doxycycline can often be taken with food if it upsets your stomach, but follow your product’s dairy and mineral spacing directions.
